sections.heading: i. Current State
sections.content_instructions:
Describe how the relevant UPI flow works TODAY before this change. Explain the existing mechanism, who the participants are, and how the current process operates. Minimum 2 paragraphs. Business language only — no XML tags or API field names.

Feature: Smart Amount Suggestion for Payments.

AUTHORITATIVE SPECIFICATIONS for this section (NPCI corpus — do NOT invent alternatives):
Current State: Today a UPI payment follows a fixed API sequence: the payer PSP sends a ReqPay, NPCI requests authentication details from the payee PSP, the payee validates and returns authentication, and finally the payer PSP confirms the transaction. The amount is entered manually by the user and is validated against static limits (e.g., >₹15,000 requires PIN, >₹500 for UPI‑Lite offline). No UI‑level suggestion of amounts exists in the protocol.
Limitations / Pain Points: Manual entry of the transaction amount leads to longer payment times and higher probability of typographical errors. Payers also lack quick access to historically frequent amounts, which can cause friction especially for repeat contacts or merchants.
RULE: Business language only. No XML, no XSD field names.

PM Clarification Answers (authoritative — override assumptions):
Q: **[Required]** We’re assuming the payer will be identified by a **Virtual Payment Address (VPA)**, which is the standard identifier used in existing UPI flows. Confirm, or specify an alternative identifier type (e.g., mobile number, Aadhaar‑linked ID).
A: yes use VPA

Q: **[Required]** We assume the payee will be identified by a **VPA (or merchant‑specific VPA/merchant ID)**, consistent with current person‑to‑person and merchant transactions. Confirm, or indicate if a different payee identifier (e.g., QR‑code ID, merchant code) is required.
A: yes continue with VPA

Q: **[Required]** For the Smart Amount Suggestions flow we propose to enforce the **existing per‑transaction limits** – ₹15,000 for standard UPI and ₹500 for UPI‑Lite – on any suggested amount. Confirm, or provide a distinct per‑transaction limit that should apply to suggested amounts.
A: yes

Q: **[Required]** We assume the **daily aggregate limits** remain the same as today (e.g., ₹1 Lakh per day for standard UPI, ₹10 K per day for UPI‑Lite) and will apply to transactions initiated via suggestions. Confirm, or specify separate daily limits for the suggestion feature.
A: yes

Q: **[Required]** To support the UI‑level suggestion capability we suggest adding a **new API message “GetAmountSuggestions”** (request: payerVPA, payeeVPA, maxSuggestions; response: up to three suggested_amounts, optional confidence_score). Confirm this design, or propose an alternative API name/structure (e.g., “FetchSuggestedAmounts”, additional fields, etc.).
A: yes continue with this
sections.prompt_instruction: Write 2 paragraphs: (1) the existing process and participants, (2) how the current system handles the relevant scenario.

sections.content_instructions:
Explain why the current state is insufficient. Cover security gaps, friction points, scalability concerns, or regulatory gaps. Use bullet points or numbered items for each limitation. Minimum 1 paragraph + 4 bullet points.

sections.heading: i. Product Description
sections.content_instructions:
Provide a high-level description of what this feature/product does and what business outcome it achieves. Name the affected participants and the scope of changes. Business language only. Minimum 2 paragraphs.

Feature: Smart Amount Suggestion for Payments.

AUTHORITATIVE TECHNICAL SPECIFICATIONS (derived from NPCI corpus — do NOT invent alternatives):
Current State (use in Background section):
  Today a UPI payment follows a fixed API sequence: the payer PSP sends a ReqPay, NPCI requests authentication details from the payee PSP, the payee validates and returns authentication, and finally the payer PSP confirms the transaction. The amount is entered manually by the user and is validated against static limits (e.g., >₹15,000 requires PIN, >₹500 for UPI‑Lite offline). No UI‑level suggestion of amounts exists in the protocol.
Limitations (use in Background section):
  Manual entry of the transaction amount leads to longer payment times and higher probability of typographical errors. Payers also lack quick access to historically frequent amounts, which can cause friction especially for repeat contacts or merchants.
Functional Requirements:
  FR-01: The app shall retrieve the payer's last N transaction amounts for a given VPA from the PSP's transaction history.
  FR-02: The app shall display up to three amount suggestions derived from the retrieved history on the payment screen.
  FR-03: The payer shall be able to select a suggested amount with a single tap.
  FR-04: The payer shall be able to edit the suggested amount before confirming the payment.
  FR-05: The selected (or edited) amount shall be passed unchanged in the ReqPay message to NPCI.
  FR-06: The system shall enforce existing amount limits (₹15,000 for standard UPI, ₹500 for UPI‑Lite) on the suggested amount.
Dispute Framework:
  Standard UDIR dispute and chargeback processes remain applicable unchanged; the Smart Amount Suggestions feature does not introduce new dispute types.
Participant Obligations:
  PSP Banks: must implement the UI to fetch and display amount suggestions from their transaction repository; must validate the final amount against existing UPI limits before sending ReqPay
  Issuer Banks: must continue to enforce PIN verification for amounts above the defined thresholds; must respond to standard UPI APIs without modification
  NPCI: will route ReqPay, ReqAuthDetails and related messages unchanged; will validate amount limits as per existing specifications

Flow Sequence (business language):
  ["Step 1: Payer PSP \u2014 initiates ReqPay with amount, payerVPA, payeeVPA, purposeCode", "Step 2: NPCI \u2014 forwards ReqAuthDetails to Payee PSP", "Step 3: Payee PSP \u2014 validates merchant details, returns RespAuthDetails", "Step 4: Payer PSP \u2014 sends ReqTxnConfirmation (or ReqDebit for voucher) to NPCI", "Step 5: NPCI \u2014 processes debit, returns RespTxnConfirmation to Payer PSP"]
RULE: Business language only. No XML, no XSD field names.

PM Clarification Answers (authoritative — override assumptions):
Q: **[Required]** We’re assuming the payer will be identified by a **Virtual Payment Address (VPA)**, which is the standard identifier used in existing UPI flows. Confirm, or specify an alternative identifier type (e.g., mobile number, Aadhaar‑linked ID).
A: yes use VPA

Q: **[Required]** We assume the payee will be identified by a **VPA (or merchant‑specific VPA/merchant ID)**, consistent with current person‑to‑person and merchant transactions. Confirm, or indicate if a different payee identifier (e.g., QR‑code ID, merchant code) is required.
A: yes continue with VPA

Q: **[Required]** For the Smart Amount Suggestions flow we propose to enforce the **existing per‑transaction limits** – ₹15,000 for standard UPI and ₹500 for UPI‑Lite – on any suggested amount. Confirm, or provide a distinct per‑transaction limit that should apply to suggested amounts.
A: yes

Q: **[Required]** We assume the **daily aggregate limits** remain the same as today (e.g., ₹1 Lakh per day for standard UPI, ₹10 K per day for UPI‑Lite) and will apply to transactions initiated via suggestions. Confirm, or specify separate daily limits for the suggestion feature.
A: yes

Q: **[Required]** To support the UI‑level suggestion capability we suggest adding a **new API message “GetAmountSuggestions”** (request: payerVPA, payeeVPA, maxSuggestions; response: up to three suggested_amounts, optional confidence_score). Confirm this design, or propose an alternative API name/structure (e.g., “FetchSuggestedAmounts”, additional fields, etc.).
A: yes continue with this
sections.prompt_instruction: Write 2-3 paragraphs describing: what the feature does, who it affects, and what problem it solves from a business perspective. No API details.

sections.heading: ii. Product Construct: Setting / Enrollment
sections.content_instructions:
If the feature requires explicit enrollment or opt-in: describe UI placement, consent capture, and the step-by-step enrollment journey with a Roles & Responsibilities table [Step, Activity, Responsible]. If the feature does NOT require a separate enrollment or setting step: write exactly two sentences stating that this section is not applicable for this feature and briefly explain why (e.g., 'This feature does not require a separate enrollment step. Users access it directly from [entry point] without prior registration or opt-in.'). NEVER leave this section blank.

Feature: Smart Amount Suggestion for Payments.

AUTHORITATIVE SPECIFICATIONS for this section (NPCI corpus — do NOT invent alternatives):
APIs: [
  {
    "name": "ReqPay",
    "response": "RespPay",
    "initiator": "Payer PSP",
    "description": "Initiates a payment request with amount and VPA details"
  },
  {
    "name": "ReqAuthDetails",
    "response": "RespAuthDetails",
    "initiator": "NPCI",
    "description": "Requests authentication details from Payee PSP"
  },
  {
    "name": "ReqTxnConfirmation",
    "response": "RespTxnConfirmation",
    "initiator": "Payer PSP",
    "description": "Confirms transaction after authentication"
  },
  {
    "name": "ReqDebit",
    "response": "RespDebit",
    "initiator": "NPCI",
    "description": "Debits the payer account/voucher as per approved request"
  }
]
Flow Sequence:
  Step 1: Payer PSP — initiates ReqPay with amount, payerVPA, payeeVPA, purposeCode
  Step 2: NPCI — forwards ReqAuthDetails to Payee PSP
  Step 3: Payee PSP — validates merchant details, returns RespAuthDetails
  Step 4: Payer PSP — sends ReqTxnConfirmation (or ReqDebit for voucher) to NPCI
  Step 5: NPCI — processes debit, returns RespTxnConfirmation to Payer PSP
Auth Method: UPI PIN
Transaction Limit: INR 15,000 per transaction for standard UPI (PIN required for >15,000); INR 500 for UPI‑Lite offline transactions (PIN required for >500)
User Journey:
  Step 1: User opens the PSP app, selects a contact or merchant VPA.
  Step 2: PSP app fetches recent transaction amounts for that VPA and shows up to three suggestions.
  Step 3: User taps a suggested amount (or edits it) and proceeds.
  Step 4: PSP app sends ReqPay with the chosen amount to NPCI.
  Step 5: NPCI follows the normal authentication and confirmation flow and returns the final status.
RULE: Business language only. No XML, no XSD field names.

PM Clarification Answers (authoritative — override assumptions):
Q: **[Required]** We’re assuming the payer will be identified by a **Virtual Payment Address (VPA)**, which is the standard identifier used in existing UPI flows. Confirm, or specify an alternative identifier type (e.g., mobile number, Aadhaar‑linked ID).
A: yes use VPA

Q: **[Required]** We assume the payee will be identified by a **VPA (or merchant‑specific VPA/merchant ID)**, consistent with current person‑to‑person and merchant transactions. Confirm, or indicate if a different payee identifier (e.g., QR‑code ID, merchant code) is required.
A: yes continue with VPA

Q: **[Required]** For the Smart Amount Suggestions flow we propose to enforce the **existing per‑transaction limits** – ₹15,000 for standard UPI and ₹500 for UPI‑Lite – on any suggested amount. Confirm, or provide a distinct per‑transaction limit that should apply to suggested amounts.
A: yes

Q: **[Required]** We assume the **daily aggregate limits** remain the same as today (e.g., ₹1 Lakh per day for standard UPI, ₹10 K per day for UPI‑Lite) and will apply to transactions initiated via suggestions. Confirm, or specify separate daily limits for the suggestion feature.
A: yes

Q: **[Required]** To support the UI‑level suggestion capability we suggest adding a **new API message “GetAmountSuggestions”** (request: payerVPA, payeeVPA, maxSuggestions; response: up to three suggested_amounts, optional confidence_score). Confirm this design, or propose an alternative API name/structure (e.g., “FetchSuggestedAmounts”, additional fields, etc.).
A: yes continue with this

sections.heading: Other Salient Points
sections.content_instructions:
List edge cases, constraints, opt-in/opt-out rules, backward-compatibility requirements, and any operational rules not covered in the Product Construct sections. Minimum 6 numbered points. Each point is one self-contained business rule or constraint.

Feature: Smart Amount Suggestion for Payments.

AUTHORITATIVE SPECIFICATIONS for this section (NPCI corpus — do NOT invent alternatives):
Policy Rules:
  Rule 1: Suggested amounts must not exceed the statutory per‑transaction limit; if they do, the app shall automatically truncate or hide the suggestion.
  Rule 2: The suggestion engine shall retain transaction history for a maximum of 12 months in compliance with data retention policies.
RULE: Business language only. No XML, no XSD field names.

sections.prompt_instruction: Write a short intro paragraph, then produce a table with columns [ID, Requirement, Priority]. IDs start at FR-01. Priority values: High / Medium / Low. Each requirement is a single testable statement starting with 'The system shall'.

sections.heading: A. NPCI (UPI Platform & CL) — Setting / Schema / Registration Changes
sections.content_instructions:
List all NPCI platform, Common Library (CL), and schema changes required for the enrollment/setting flow. Number each requirement. Cover: new API messages, changed schema fields, CL version changes, configuration flags. Minimum 2 paragraphs + ≥ 4 numbered requirements.

Feature: Smart Amount Suggestion for Payments.

AUTHORITATIVE SPECIFICATIONS for this section (NPCI corpus — do NOT invent alternatives):
User Journey:
  Step 1: User opens the PSP app, selects a contact or merchant VPA.
  Step 2: PSP app fetches recent transaction amounts for that VPA and shows up to three suggestions.
  Step 3: User taps a suggested amount (or edits it) and proceeds.
  Step 4: PSP app sends ReqPay with the chosen amount to NPCI.
  Step 5: NPCI follows the normal authentication and confirmation flow and returns the final status.
RULE: Business language only. No XML, no XSD field names.

PM Clarification Answers (authoritative — override assumptions):
Q: **[Required]** We’re assuming the payer will be identified by a **Virtual Payment Address (VPA)**, which is the standard identifier used in existing UPI flows. Confirm, or specify an alternative identifier type (e.g., mobile number, Aadhaar‑linked ID).
A: yes use VPA

Q: **[Required]** We assume the payee will be identified by a **VPA (or merchant‑specific VPA/merchant ID)**, consistent with current person‑to‑person and merchant transactions. Confirm, or indicate if a different payee identifier (e.g., QR‑code ID, merchant code) is required.
A: yes continue with VPA

Q: **[Required]** For the Smart Amount Suggestions flow we propose to enforce the **existing per‑transaction limits** – ₹15,000 for standard UPI and ₹500 for UPI‑Lite – on any suggested amount. Confirm, or provide a distinct per‑transaction limit that should apply to suggested amounts.
A: yes

Q: **[Required]** We assume the **daily aggregate limits** remain the same as today (e.g., ₹1 Lakh per day for standard UPI, ₹10 K per day for UPI‑Lite) and will apply to transactions initiated via suggestions. Confirm, or specify separate daily limits for the suggestion feature.
A: yes

Q: **[Required]** To support the UI‑level suggestion capability we suggest adding a **new API message “GetAmountSuggestions”** (request: payerVPA, payeeVPA, maxSuggestions; response: up to three suggested_amounts, optional confidence_score). Confirm this design, or propose an alternative API name/structure (e.g., “FetchSuggestedAmounts”, additional fields, etc.).
A: yes continue with this
sections.prompt_instruction: Write a short intro, then number each change: (1) what changes, (2) which API or schema, (3) which participant must implement it. Business language — no raw XML tags.

sections.heading: B. NPCI (UPI Platform & CL) — Transaction Flow / Processing Changes
sections.content_instructions:
List all NPCI routing, processing, and validation changes for the payment transaction flow. Number each requirement. Cover: refCategory handling, credential validation, switch routing changes, response handling. Minimum 2 paragraphs + ≥ 4 numbered requirements.

Feature: Smart Amount Suggestion for Payments.

AUTHORITATIVE SPECIFICATIONS for this section (NPCI corpus — do NOT invent alternatives):
APIs: [
  {
    "name": "ReqPay",
    "response": "RespPay",
    "initiator": "Payer PSP",
    "description": "Initiates a payment request with amount and VPA details"
  },
  {
    "name": "ReqAuthDetails",
    "response": "RespAuthDetails",
    "initiator": "NPCI",
    "description": "Requests authentication details from Payee PSP"
  },
  {
    "name": "ReqTxnConfirmation",
    "response": "RespTxnConfirmation",
    "initiator": "Payer PSP",
    "description": "Confirms transaction after authentication"
  },
  {
    "name": "ReqDebit",
    "response": "RespDebit",
    "initiator": "NPCI",
    "description": "Debits the payer account/voucher as per approved request"
  }
]
Flow Sequence:
  Step 1: Payer PSP — initiates ReqPay with amount, payerVPA, payeeVPA, purposeCode
  Step 2: NPCI — forwards ReqAuthDetails to Payee PSP
  Step 3: Payee PSP — validates merchant details, returns RespAuthDetails
  Step 4: Payer PSP — sends ReqTxnConfirmation (or ReqDebit for voucher) to NPCI
  Step 5: NPCI — processes debit, returns RespTxnConfirmation to Payer PSP
Auth Method: UPI PIN
Transaction Limit: INR 15,000 per transaction for standard UPI (PIN required for >15,000); INR 500 for UPI‑Lite offline transactions (PIN required for >500)
RULE: Business language only. No XML, no XSD field names.

PM Clarification Answers (authoritative — override assumptions):

A: yes

Q: **[Required]** To support the UI‑level suggestion capability we suggest adding a **new API message “GetAmountSuggestions”** (request: payerVPA, payeeVPA, maxSuggestions; response: up to three suggested_amounts, optional confidence_score). Confirm this design, or propose an alternative API name/structure (e.g., “FetchSuggestedAmounts”, additional fields, etc.).

sections.heading: A. UPI App / PSP — App-side Changes
sections.content_instructions:
List all UPI App and PSP changes required on the app side for enrollment, consent capture, and device binding. Number each requirement. Cover: UI changes, CL SDK invocation, local key storage, device check logic. Minimum 2 paragraphs + ≥ 4 numbered requirements.

Feature: Smart Amount Suggestion for Payments.

AUTHORITATIVE TECHNICAL SPECIFICATIONS (derived from NPCI corpus — do NOT invent alternatives):
Current State (use in Background section):
  Today a UPI payment follows a fixed API sequence: the payer PSP sends a ReqPay, NPCI requests authentication details from the payee PSP, the payee validates and returns authentication, and finally the payer PSP confirms the transaction. The amount is entered manually by the user and is validated against static limits (e.g., >₹15,000 requires PIN, >₹500 for UPI‑Lite offline). No UI‑level suggestion of amounts exists in the protocol.
Limitations (use in Background section):
  Manual entry of the transaction amount leads to longer payment times and higher probability of typographical errors. Payers also lack quick access to historically frequent amounts, which can cause friction especially for repeat contacts or merchants.
Functional Requirements:
  FR-01: The app shall retrieve the payer's last N transaction amounts for a given VPA from the PSP's transaction history.
  FR-02: The app shall display up to three amount suggestions derived from the retrieved history on the payment screen.
  FR-03: The payer shall be able to select a suggested amount with a single tap.
  FR-04: The payer shall be able to edit the suggested amount before confirming the payment.
  FR-05: The selected (or edited) amount shall be passed unchanged in the ReqPay message to NPCI.
  FR-06: The system shall enforce existing amount limits (₹15,000 for standard UPI, ₹500 for UPI‑Lite) on the suggested amount.
Dispute Framework:
  Standard UDIR dispute and chargeback processes remain applicable unchanged; the Smart Amount Suggestions feature does not introduce new dispute types.
Participant Obligations:
  PSP Banks: must implement the UI to fetch and display amount suggestions from their transaction repository; must validate the final amount against existing UPI limits before sending ReqPay
  Issuer Banks: must continue to enforce PIN verification for amounts above the defined thresholds; must respond to standard UPI APIs without modification
  NPCI: will route ReqPay, ReqAuthDetails and related messages unchanged; will validate amount limits as per existing specifications

Flow Sequence (business language):
  ["Step 1: Payer PSP \u2014 initiates ReqPay with amount, payerVPA, payeeVPA, purposeCode", "Step 2: NPCI \u2014 forwards ReqAuthDetails to Payee PSP", "Step 3: Payee PSP \u2014 validates merchant details, returns RespAuthDetails", "Step 4: Payer PSP \u2014 sends ReqTxnConfirmation (or ReqDebit for voucher) to NPCI", "Step 5: NPCI \u2014 processes debit, returns RespTxnConfirmation to Payer PSP"]
RULE: Business language only. No XML, no XSD field names.

PM Clarification Answers (authoritative — override assumptions):
Q: **[Required]** We’re assuming the payer will be identified by a **Virtual Payment Address (VPA)**, which is the standard identifier used in existing UPI flows. Confirm, or specify an alternative identifier type (e.g., mobile number, Aadhaar‑linked ID).
A: yes use VPA

Q: **[Required]** We assume the payee will be identified by a **VPA (or merchant‑specific VPA/merchant ID)**, consistent with current person‑to‑person and merchant transactions. Confirm, or indicate if a different payee identifier (e.g., QR‑code ID, merchant code) is required.
A: yes continue with VPA

Q: **[Required]** For the Smart Amount Suggestions flow we propose to enforce the **existing per‑transaction limits** – ₹15,000 for standard UPI and ₹500 for UPI‑Lite – on any suggested amount. Confirm, or provide a distinct per‑transaction limit that should apply to suggested amounts.
A: yes

Q: **[Required]** We assume the **daily aggregate limits** remain the same as today (e.g., ₹1 Lakh per day for standard UPI, ₹10 K per day for UPI‑Lite) and will apply to transactions initiated via suggestions. Confirm, or specify separate daily limits for the suggestion feature.
A: yes

Q: **[Required]** To support the UI‑level suggestion capability we suggest adding a **new API message “GetAmountSuggestions”** (request: payerVPA, payeeVPA, maxSuggestions; response: up to three suggested_amounts, optional confidence_score). Confirm this design, or propose an alternative API name/structure (e.g., “FetchSuggestedAmounts”, additional fields, etc.).
A: yes continue with this
